visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents Required
All original documents must be present when submitting the visa application at VFS Austria Jakarta and Bali.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here
- For Austria
- For Malta
- For Slovenia
- For Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein)

For Austria
AUSTRIAN EMBASSY
Jakarta
Schengen tourist visa
Documents provided by the applicant
- Visa application form, completed and signed (both parents need to sign on minor’s visa application form) with 2 attached recent photos (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted person, must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid 3 months longer than the expiry date of the visa).
- Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa, UK Visa, US Visa (if available)
- Individual tourists:

- Flight reservation, (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only). Please bear in mind Austria must be the main destination. You do not need to buy the ticket to apply for the visa: a reservation is accepted. However, you will be requested to submit the actual ticket before the visa is actually issued, in case the visa is approved. If the trip includes several destinations, proof of transport must also be submitted (Flight reservation must be issued by Airline with airline logo in headline).
- Hotel reservations in the Schengen member states ( Must be mentioned hotel address and all guests names in English or German only) and provide confirmation letter from each hotel in Schengen Area.
- Travel plan (tour program from travel agency or a letter by applicant stating all cities and countries to be visited and the length of stay), for Group Applications (Group Tour or Incentive Tour) need to add Master Documents consist sponsor letter from Travel Agency, name list of group tour member, itinerary, flight booking, hotel reservation in all Schengen Area and travel insurance with name list and table benefit.
- Original Personal bank account statements must cover the last 3 months and need to be under the applicant’s name. Families travelling together also need to submit bank statements for the last 3 months of every travelling family member who has a bank account, except minors (under 18 years old). In addition, if applicant’s expenses are covered by a family member or company, the respective sponsor’s last 3 months bank statements, financial sponsorship letter and photocopy of sponsor’s ID Card must be submitted with each application. Original Bank account statement must be legalized by Bank.
- Original Letter from employer or Work certificate and copy of SIUP or TDP (in case of business owner: company registration) OR Student certificate (School Letter), in case of your status is student or college student, Copy of Doctor Certification (Surat Ijin Praktek or Ikatan Dokter Indonesia card) in case medical doctor,
Work or student certificate presented have to be translated into English or German) And Original Pay Slip for the last 3 months.
- Travel insurance (minimum coverage 30,000 €; policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company) and should cover Covid. Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies.
- For underage children under 18 years old to submit application : Document is required; birth certificate, house registration, the authorization of the change of the surname and first name ( if changed) and consent letter have to issue from district or public notary (if travel with a single parent).
- Copy of the Family Card, Marriage Certificate and Birth Certificate (when traveling with family).
In case of a private invitation, if the applicant has not enough own financial means: electronic guarantee letter, which can be applied for at the local police station, where the sponsor is residing. For more information please visit:https://www.bmi.gv.at/202/Fremdenpolizei_und_Grenzkontrolle/Einreise/start.aspx#eve (https://www.bmi.gv.at/202/Fremdenpolizei_und_Grenzkontrolle/Einreise/start.aspx#eve)
In case of a round trip through several Schengen Member States the visa application has to be submitted to the Embassy of the country where the longest stay is intended. In case of equal length of stay in all visited Schengen Member States the application has to be presented at the Embassy of the country which will be entered first.The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Note : all documents must be printed in A4 format.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant and/or submission of falsified or forged documents will result in the refusal of the visa application.

Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein)
Documents provided by the applicant
- Full Marriage/Full Birth certificates, etc. must be submitted with application as proof of relationship with EU/EEA spouse. Also provide air ticket which clearly shows that you will be traveling with the EU/EEA passport holder.
- Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photos (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted person, must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid 3 months longer than the expiry date of the visa).
- Recommendation: Travel Insurance (minimum coverage 30,000 €; policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company) and should cover Covid. Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant and/or submission of falsified or forged documents will result in the refusal of the visa application.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[Table:  Tourist
                                                                        Visa]
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in IDR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change every month end.
- All the fees are non-refundable.
- There is a service charge of IDR 415,000 levied on per application over and above the visa fees.
- Courier facility available at IDR 81,000 (Zone 1), 153,000 (Zone 2), 190,000 (Zone 3) per application.
- SMS facility available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Call Back Service available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Form Filling Facility available at IDR 150,000/- per application.
- Premium Lounge Facility available at IDR 1,162,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Appointment facility available at IDR 707,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Passport Collection facility available at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Document Printing facility available at IDR 13,000/- per page.
- Photograph facility available at IDR 58,000/- per application.
- SIM Card facility available at IDR 345,000/- per application.
- Extended Hours Passport Collection at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Jakarta Area at IDR 150,000/- per application (Jakarta Only).
- Courier Assurance at IDR 273,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 1 + SMS at IDR 75,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 2 + SMS at IDR 132,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 3 + SMS at IDR 190,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Plus (SMS + Courier Return + Courier Assurance) at IDR 157,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Shipment at IDR 255,000/- per application (Bali Only)
- The visa fee for applicants applying to the Austria Visa Application Centre is payable by cash.
- VFS charges (service charges & other optional charges) are to be paid in cash only.
- All VFS service charges are inclusive of Value Added Tax (VAT).
Visas are issued free of charge for:
- Children under age 6
- School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research
- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein) and their children, under the age 21 – under certain circumstances (please verify with the relevant consular authority). On the condition that the marriage is recognized by the country of the EU partner. Original of spouses’ EU/EEA passport, Full Marriage/Full Birth certificates, etc. must be submitted with application as proof of relationship with EU/EEA spouse/parent. Also provide air ticket which clearly shows that you will be traveling with the EU/EEA passport holder.
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

Due to a bilateral agreement the visa application fee for applicants of the following countries amount to € 35: Russian Federation, Ukraine, Albania, Bosnia-Herzegovina, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia and Moldova
The visa fee is not refundable if a negative decision is taken on the application or if the application is withdrawn.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is
                                                                    minimum 15 days (starting from the time when the
                                                                    Embassy or Consulate received the application, not
                                                                    including the transportation time), counting from
                                                                    when all requested documents have been submitted.
                                                                    This period may be extended in specific cases,
                                                                    notably when further scrutiny of the application is
                                                                    required, for example, when an applicant must come
                                                                    to an interview or submit additional documentation
                                                                    requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During
                                                                    certain times of the year, the processing time can
                                                                    be longer due to the high volume of applications. It
                                                                    is recommended to submit your visa application at
                                                                    least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if
                                                                    possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
                                                                
Please note that the application processing time and
                                                                    decisions on visa applications are the sole
                                                                    prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global
                                                                    has no role to play in these areas of the
                                                                    application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Tourist Visa is given to those visiting Austria for
                                                                    tourism or other non-business related purposes.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Tourist Visa is given to those visiting Austria for
                                                                    tourism or other non-business related purposes.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[Table:  Tourist
                                                                        Visa]
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in IDR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change every month end.
- All the fees are non-refundable.
- There is a service charge of IDR 415,000 levied on per application over and above the visa fees.
- Courier facility available at IDR 81,000 (Zone 1), 153,000 (Zone 2), 190,000 (Zone 3) per application.
- SMS facility available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Call Back Service available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Form Filling Facility available at IDR 150,000/- per application.
- Premium Lounge Facility available at IDR 1,162,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Appointment facility available at IDR 707,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Passport Collection facility available at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Document Printing facility available at IDR 13,000/- per page.
- Photograph facility available at IDR 58,000/- per application.
- SIM Card facility available at IDR 345,000/- per application.
- Extended Hours Passport Collection at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Jakarta Area at IDR 150,000/- per application (Jakarta Only).
- Courier Assurance at IDR 273,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 1 + SMS at IDR 75,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 2 + SMS at IDR 132,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 3 + SMS at IDR 190,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Plus (SMS + Courier Return + Courier Assurance) at IDR 157,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Shipment at IDR 255,000/- per application (Bali Only)
- The visa fee for applicants applying to the Austria Visa Application Centre is payable by cash.
- VFS charges (service charges & other optional charges) are to be paid in cash only.
- All VFS service charges are inclusive of Value Added Tax (VAT).
Visas are issued free of charge for:
- Children under age 6
- School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research
- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein) and their children, under the age 21 – under certain circumstances (please verify with the relevant consular authority). On the condition that the marriage is recognized by the country of the EU partner. Original of spouses’ EU/EEA passport, Full Marriage/Full Birth certificates, etc. must be submitted with application as proof of relationship with EU/EEA spouse/parent. Also provide air ticket which clearly shows that you will be traveling with the EU/EEA passport holder.
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

Due to a bilateral agreement the visa application fee for applicants of the following countries amount to € 35: Russian Federation, Ukraine, Albania, Bosnia-Herzegovina, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia and Moldova
The visa fee is not refundable if a negative decision is taken on the application or if the application is withdrawn.

## Documents Required
Documents Required
All original documents must be present when submitting the visa application at VFS Austria Jakarta and Bali.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here
- For Austria
- For Malta
- For Slovenia
- For Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein)

For Austria

Jakarta
Schengen tourist visa

The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Note : all documents must be printed in A4 format.

Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein)
Documents provided by the applicant
- Full Marriage/Full Birth certificates, etc. must be submitted with application as proof of relationship with EU/EEA spouse. Also provide air ticket which clearly shows that you will be traveling with the EU/EEA passport holder.
- Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photos (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted person, must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid 3 months longer than the expiry date of the visa).
- Recommendation: Travel Insurance (minimum coverage 30,000 €; policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company) and should cover Covid. Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant and/or submission of falsified or forged documents will result in the refusal of the visa application.

## For Malta
Documents provided by the applicant
- Visa application form, completed and signed (both parents need to sign on minor’s visa application form), with 2 attached recent photos (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted person, must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid three months longer than the expiry date of the visa).
- Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa, UK Visa, US Visa (if available)
- Individual tourists:

- Flight reservation, (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only). Please bear in mind Malta must be the main destination. You do not need to buy the ticket to apply for the visa: a reservation is accepted. However, you will be requested to submit the actual ticket before the visa is actually issued, in case the visa is approved. If the trip includes several destinations, proof of transport must also be submitted ( Flight reservation must be issued by Airline with airline logo in headline).
- Hotel reservations in the Schengen member states ( Must be mentioned hotel address and all guests names in English or German only) and provide confirmation letter from each hotel in Schengen Area.
- Travel plan (tour program from travel agency or a letter by applicant stating all cities and countries to be visited and the length of stay), for Group Applications (Group Tour or Incentive Tour) need to add Master Documents consist sponsor letter from Travel Agency, name list of group tour member, itinerary, flight booking, hotel reservation in all Schengen Area and travel insurance with name list and table benefit.
- Original Personal bank account statements must cover for the last 3 months and need to be under the applicant’s name. Families travelling together also need to submit bank statements for the last 3 months of every travelling family member who has a bank account, except minors (under 18 years old). In addition, if applicant’s expenses are covered by a family member or company, the respective sponsor’s last 3 months bank statements, financial sponsorship letter and photocopy of sponsor’s ID Card must be submitted with each application. Original Bank account statement must be legalized by Bank.
- Original Letter from employer or Work certificate and copy of SIUP or TDP (in case of business owner: company registration) OR Student certificate (School Letter), in case of your status is student or college student, Copy of Doctor Certification (Surat Ijin Praktek or Ikatan Dokter Indonesia card) in case medical doctor,
Work or student certificate presented have to be translated into English or German). And Original Pay Slip for the last 3 months.
- Travel insurance (minimum coverage 30,000 €; policy must be issued by an accredited insurance companies) and should cover Covid, Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies
- For underage children under 20 years old to submit application : Document is required; birth certificate, house registration, the authorization of the change of the surname and first name ( if changed) and consent letter have to issue from district (if travel with a single parent).
- Copy of the Family Card, Marriage Certificate and Birth Certificate (when traveling with family).
In case of a private invitation, proof that accommodation has been secured or an invitation letter, duly legalized, from the host resident in Malta and public notary. The invitation letter must submit in ORIGINAL and include the host’s name and surname, address, telephone number, and must be accompanied by copies of the host’s Maltese ID Card and passport. The signature on the invitation must be appropriately legalized. The guarantor may be asked to enclose documents with the letter of guarantee substantiating statements included in the letter of guarantee: for download Letter of Guarantee for private invitation to Malta please click here. Bank statements for the last 3 months – only if they guarantee accommodation costs in Malta for a third-country national who does not have sufficient funds;
Extract from the land register or purchase contract or apartment rental contract to prove private accommodation for the third-country national in Malta.
In case of a round trip through several Schengen member states the visa application has to be submitted to the Embassy of the country where the longest stay is intended. In case of equal length of stay in all visited Schengen member states the application has to be presented at the Embassy of the country which will be entered first.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant and/or submission of falsified or forged documents will result in the refusal of the visa application.

## For Slovenia
Documents provided by the applicant
- Visa application form, completed and signed (both parents need to sign on minor’s visa application form), with 2 attached recent photos (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted person, must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid three months longer than the expiry date of the visa).
- Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa, UK Visa, US Visa (if available)
- Individual tourists:

- Flight reservation, (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only). Please bear in mind Slovenia must be the main destination. You do not need to buy the ticket to apply for the visa: a reservation is accepted. However, you will be requested to submit the actual ticket before the visa is actually issued, in case the visa is approved. If the trip includes several destinations, proof of transport must also be submitted ( Flight reservation must be issued by Airline with airline logo in headline).
- Hotel reservations in the Schengen member states ( Must be mentioned hotel address and all guests names in English or German only) and provide confirmation letter from each hotel in Schengen Area.
- Travel plan (tour program from travel agency or a letter by applicant stating all cities and countries to be visited and the length of stay), for Group Applications (Group Tour or Incentive Tour) need to add Master Documents consist sponsor letter from Travel Agency, name list of group tour member, itinerary, flight booking, hotel reservation in all Schengen Area and travel insurance with name list and table benefit.
- Original Personal bank account statements must cover the last 3 months and need to be under the applicant’s name. Families travelling together also need to submit bank statements for the last 3 months of every travelling family member who has a bank account, except minors (under 18 years old). In addition, if applicant’s expenses are covered by a family member or company, the respective sponsor’s last 3 months bank statements, financial sponsorship letter and photocopy of sponsor’s ID Card must be submitted with each application. Original Bank account statement must be legalized by Bank.
- Original Letter from employer or Work certificate and copy of SIUP or TDP (in case of business owner: company registration) OR Student certificate (School Letter), in case of your status is student or college student, Copy of Doctor Certification (Surat Ijin Praktek or Ikatan Dokter Indonesia card) in case medical doctor,
Work or student certificate presented have to be translated into English or German) And Original Pay Slip for the last 3 months.
- Travel insurance (minimum coverage 30,000) policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company and should cover covid. (Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies.
- For underage children under 20 years old to submit application : Document is required; birth certificate, house registration, the authorization of the change of the surname and first name ( if changed) and consent letter have to issue from district (if travel with a single parent).
- Copy of the Family Card, Marriage Certificate and Birth Certificate (when traveling with family).
In case of a private invitation, Guarantee letter, must submit in ORIGINAL and certified by public notary or municipal authorities of the Republic of Slovenia, which must not be older than 30 days, and its copy.
The guarantor may be asked to enclose documents with the letter of guarantee substantiating statements included in the letter of guarantee:
Bank statements for the last 3 months – only if they guarantee accommodation costs in the Republic of Slovenia for a third-country national who does not have sufficient funds;
Extract from the land register or purchase contract or apartment rental contract – to prove private accommodation for the third-country national in the Republic of Slovenia.
Passport copy.
In case of a round trip through several Schengen member states the visa application has to be submitted to the Embassy of the country where the longest stay is intended. In case of equal length of stay in all visited Schengen member states the application has to be presented at the Embassy of the country which will be entered first.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant and/or submission of falsified or forged documents will result in the refusal of the visa application.

Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein)
Documents provided by the applicant
- Full Marriage/Full Birth certificates, etc. must be submitted with application as proof of relationship with EU/EEA spouse. Also provide air ticket which clearly shows that you will be traveling with the EU/EEA passport holder.
- Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photos (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted person, must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid 3 months longer than the expiry date of the visa)
- Recommendation: Travel insurance (minimum coverage 30,000 €; policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company) Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant and/or submission of falsified or forged documents will result in the refusal of the visa application.
Note : all documents must be printed in A4 format.

## Photo Specifications

[Table:  Tourist
                                                                        Visa]

[Table:  Tourist
                                                                        Visa]

[Table:  Tourist
                                                                        Visa]

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is
                                                                    minimum 15 days (starting from the time when the
                                                                    Embassy or Consulate received the application, not
                                                                    including the transportation time), counting from
                                                                    when all requested documents have been submitted.
                                                                    This period may be extended in specific cases,
                                                                    notably when further scrutiny of the application is
                                                                    required, for example, when an applicant must come
                                                                    to an interview or submit additional documentation
                                                                    requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During
                                                                    certain times of the year, the processing time can
                                                                    be longer due to the high volume of applications. It
                                                                    is recommended to submit your visa application at
                                                                    least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if
                                                                    possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
                                                                
Please note that the application processing time and
                                                                    decisions on visa applications are the sole
                                                                    prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global
                                                                    has no role to play in these areas of the
                                                                    application process.

## Download Form
Application Form Visa C in German, English and Bahasa please click here 
Letter of Guarantee Form for Visiting Family or Friend in Malta please click here 
As per instruction received from the Austrian Embassy applicant needs to sign on application form. Representative or travel agent must bring signed application form at the time of submission. 
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
All original documents must be present when submitting the visa application at VFS Austria Jakarta and Bali.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here
Schengen visa for Student purposes
Documents provided by the applicant:
- a. Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photographs, (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted, person must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- b. Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid three months longer than the expiry date of the requested visa).
- c. Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa (if available)
- d. Travel Insurance (minimum coverage 30,000) policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company.), Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies
- e. Flight reservation (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only).
- f. Proof of accommodation in Austria or letter of acceptance at the school’s / college’s / university’s dormitory.
- g. Confirmation of present student status e.g. original letter from school / college / university.
- h. Original and copy of “Letter of Admission” from school / college / university in Austria.
- i. Original Proof of sufficient funds to cover overall cost of living during the duration of study in Austria.
Visa “C” (“Studium”) applies for validity up to 90 days. Visa “D” (“Studium”) between 91 and 180 days. Visa in both categories cannot be extended or renewed in Austria. If applicant intends to stay longer than 6 (six) months, Residence Permit (“Aufenthaltstitel”) shall be applied for.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application. All Indonesian documents have to be translated into German or English.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant or submission of falsified or forged documents result in the refusal of the visa application.
Note : all documents must be printed in A4 format.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Student
                                                                        Visa]
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in IDR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change every month end.
All the fees are non-refundable.
There is a service charge of IDR 415,000 levied on per application over and above the visa fees.
- Courier facility available at IDR 81,000 (Zone 1), 153,000 (Zone 2), 190,000 (Zone 3) per application.
- SMS facility available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Call Back Service available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Form Filling Facility available at IDR 150,000/- per application.
- Premium Lounge Facility available at IDR 1,162,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Appointment facility available at IDR 707,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Passport Collection facility available at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Document Printing facility available at IDR 13,000/- per page.
- Photograph facility available at IDR 58,000/- per application.
- SIM Card facility available at IDR 345,000/- per application.
- Extended Hours Passport Collection at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Jakarta Area at IDR 150,000/- per application (Jakarta Only).
- Courier Assurance at IDR 273,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 1 + SMS at IDR 75,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 2 + SMS at IDR 132,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 3 + SMS at IDR 190,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Plus (SMS + Courier Return + Courier Assurance) at IDR 157,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Shipment at IDR 255,000/- per application (Bali Only)
- The visa fee for applicants applying to the Austria Visa Application Centre is payable by cash.
- VFS charges (service charges & other optional charges) are to be paid in cash only.
- All VFS service charges are inclusive of Value Added Tax (VAT).
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is
                                                                    minimum 15 days (starting from the time when the
                                                                    Embassy or Consulate received the application, not
                                                                    including the transportation time), counting from
                                                                    when all requested documents have been submitted.
                                                                    This period may be extended in specific cases,
                                                                    notably when further scrutiny of the application is
                                                                    required, for example, when an applicant must come
                                                                    to an interview or submit additional documentation
                                                                    requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During
                                                                    certain times of the year, the processing time can
                                                                    be longer due to the high volume of applications. It
                                                                    is recommended to submit your visa application at
                                                                    least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if
                                                                    possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
                                                                
Please note that the application processing time and
                                                                    decisions on visa applications are the sole
                                                                    prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global
                                                                    has no role to play in these areas of the
                                                                    application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Student visas are given to bona fide students to pursue regular studies at recognized institutions in Austria. The visa is multiple entries and valid for the period of study as approved by the educational institution in Austria.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Student visas are given to bona fide students to pursue regular studies at recognized institutions in Austria. The visa is multiple entries and valid for the period of study as approved by the educational institution in Austria. 
                                                                

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Student
                                                                        Visa]
Please Note:
The applicable visa fee in IDR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change every month end.
All the fees are non-refundable.
There is a service charge of IDR 415,000 levied on per application over and above the visa fees.
- Courier facility available at IDR 81,000 (Zone 1), 153,000 (Zone 2), 190,000 (Zone 3) per application.
- SMS facility available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Call Back Service available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Form Filling Facility available at IDR 150,000/- per application.
- Premium Lounge Facility available at IDR 1,162,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Appointment facility available at IDR 707,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Passport Collection facility available at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Document Printing facility available at IDR 13,000/- per page.
- Photograph facility available at IDR 58,000/- per application.
- SIM Card facility available at IDR 345,000/- per application.
- Extended Hours Passport Collection at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Jakarta Area at IDR 150,000/- per application (Jakarta Only).
- Courier Assurance at IDR 273,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 1 + SMS at IDR 75,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 2 + SMS at IDR 132,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 3 + SMS at IDR 190,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Plus (SMS + Courier Return + Courier Assurance) at IDR 157,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Shipment at IDR 255,000/- per application (Bali Only)
- The visa fee for applicants applying to the Austria Visa Application Centre is payable by cash.
- VFS charges (service charges & other optional charges) are to be paid in cash only.
- All VFS service charges are inclusive of Value Added Tax (VAT).

## Terms and Conditions:
Kindly be informed that Logistic fee and other additional service fees are not refundable once the payment is made.

## Documents Required
All original documents must be present when submitting the visa application at VFS Austria Jakarta and Bali.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here
Schengen visa for Student purposes
Documents provided by the applicant:
- a. Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photographs, (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted, person must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- b. Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid three months longer than the expiry date of the requested visa).
- c. Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa (if available)
- d. Travel Insurance (minimum coverage 30,000) policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company.), Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies
- e. Flight reservation (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only).
- f. Proof of accommodation in Austria or letter of acceptance at the school’s / college’s / university’s dormitory.
- g. Confirmation of present student status e.g. original letter from school / college / university.
- h. Original and copy of “Letter of Admission” from school / college / university in Austria.
- i. Original Proof of sufficient funds to cover overall cost of living during the duration of study in Austria.
Visa “C” (“Studium”) applies for validity up to 90 days. Visa “D” (“Studium”) between 91 and 180 days. Visa in both categories cannot be extended or renewed in Austria. If applicant intends to stay longer than 6 (six) months, Residence Permit (“Aufenthaltstitel”) shall be applied for.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application. All Indonesian documents have to be translated into German or English.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant or submission of falsified or forged documents result in the refusal of the visa application.
Note : all documents must be printed in A4 format.

## Photo Specifications

                                                                        Visa]

[Table:  Student
                                                                        Visa]

[Table:  Student
                                                                        Visa]

[Table:  Student
                                                                        Visa]

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is
                                                                    minimum 15 days (starting from the time when the
                                                                    Embassy or Consulate received the application, not
                                                                    including the transportation time), counting from
                                                                    when all requested documents have been submitted.
                                                                    This period may be extended in specific cases,
                                                                    notably when further scrutiny of the application is
                                                                    required, for example, when an applicant must come
                                                                    to an interview or submit additional documentation
                                                                    requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During
                                                                    certain times of the year, the processing time can
                                                                    be longer due to the high volume of applications. It
                                                                    is recommended to submit your visa application at
                                                                    least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if
                                                                    possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.
                                                                
Please note that the application processing time and
                                                                    decisions on visa applications are the sole
                                                                    prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global
                                                                    has no role to play in these areas of the
                                                                    application process.

## Download Form

Application Form Visa C in German, English and Bahasa please click here
As per instruction received from the Austrian Embassy
                                                                    applicant needs to sign on application form.
                                                                    Representative or travel agent must bring signed
                                                                    application form at the time
                                                                    of submission. 

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your
                                                                        computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the
                                                                        required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
All original documents must be present when submitting the visa application at VFS Austria Jakarta and Bali.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here
visa_types.visa_fees:
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[Table:  Business
                                                                        Visa]
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in IDR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change every month end.
- All the fees are non-refundable.
- There is a service charge of IDR 415,000 levied on per application over and above the visa fees.
- Courier facility available at IDR 81,000 (Zone 1), 153,000 (Zone 2), 190,000 (Zone 3) per application.
- SMS facility available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Call Back Service available at IDR 41,000/- per application.
- Form Filling Facility available at IDR 150,000/- per application.
- Premium Lounge Facility available at IDR 1,162,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Appointment facility available at IDR 707,000/- per application.
- Prime Time Passport Collection facility available at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Document Printing facility available at IDR 13,000/- per page.
- Photograph facility available at IDR 58,000/- per application.
- SIM Card facility available at IDR 345,000/- per application.
- Extended Hours Passport Collection at IDR 46,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Jakarta Area at IDR 150,000/- per application (Jakarta Only).
- Courier Assurance at IDR 273,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 1 + SMS at IDR 75,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 2 + SMS at IDR 132,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Zone 3 + SMS at IDR 190,000/- per application.
- Combo Package: Courier Plus (SMS + Courier Return + Courier Assurance) at IDR 157,000/- per application.
- Express Courier Shipment at IDR 255,000/- per application (Bali Only)
- The visa fee for applicants applying to the Austria Visa Application Centre is payable by cash.
- VFS charges (service charges & other optional charges) are to be paid in cash only.
- All VFS service charges are inclusive of Value Added Tax (VAT).
Visas are issued free of charge for:
- Children under age 6.
- School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein) and their children, under the age 21 – under certain circumstances (please verify with the relevant consular authority). On the condition that the marriage is recognized by the country of the EU partner. Original of spouses’ EU/EEA passport, Full Marriage/Full Birth certificates, etc. must be submitted with application as proof of relationship with EU/EEA spouse/parent. Also provide air ticket which clearly shows that you will be traveling with the EU/EEA passport holder.
- Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

Due to a bilateral agreement the visa application fee for applicants of the following countries amount to € 35: Russian Federation, Ukraine, Albania, Bosnia-Herzegovina, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia and Moldova.
The visa fee is not refundable if a negative decision is taken on the application or if the application is withdrawn.

                                                                    application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business Visa is given those who are doing business
                                                                    in Austria such as making sales or establishing
                                                                    contacts on behalf of a company outside Indonesia.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business Visa is given those who are doing business
                                                                    in Austria such as making sales or establishing
                                                                    contacts on behalf of a company outside Indonesia.
                                                                

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[Table:  Business
                                                                        Visa]
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in IDR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change every month end.
- All the fees are non-refundable.

- Spouses of EU/EEA citizens (EEA: Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein) and their children, under the age 21 – under certain circumstances (please verify with the relevant consular authority). On the condition that the marriage is recognized by the country of the EU partner. - Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.

Due to a bilateral agreement the visa application fee for applicants of the following countries amount to € 35: Russian Federation, Ukraine, Albania, Bosnia-Herzegovina, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia and Moldova.
The visa fee is not refundable if a negative decision is taken on the application or if the application is withdrawn.

## Documents Required
All original documents must be present when submitting the visa application at VFS Austria Jakarta and Bali.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here

## Download Instructions:
- For Austria
- For Malta
- For Slovenia

## For Austria
Schengen visa for business purposes
Documents provided by the applicant: 
- Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photographs (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted, person must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid three months longer than the expiry date of the requested visa).
- Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa (if available)
- Original Letter from employer and/or sponsor stating designation, date of employment, salary, period of stay, purpose of visit, stating full responsibility for employee’s expenses during trip and his/her return stamped and signed by the General Manager (name should also be printed), and copy of SIUP or TDP (self-employed applicants should present trade/business license, tax documents etc). Copy of Doctor Certification (Surat Ijin Praktek or Ikatan Dokter Indonesia card) in case medical doctor. And Original Pay Slip for the last 3 months
- Original Personal bank account statements must cover the last 3 months and need to be under the applicant’s name. Families travelling together also need to submit bank statements for the last 3 months of every travelling family member who has a bank account, except minors (under 18 years old). In addition, if applicant’s expenses are covered by a family member or company, the respective sponsor’s last 3 months bank statements, financial sponsorship letter and photocopy of sponsor’s ID Card must be submitted with each application. Original Bank account statement must be legalized by Bank.
- Travel insurance (minimum coverage 30,000) policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company and should cover covid. (Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies.
- Flight reservation (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only) (Flight reservation must be issued by Airline with airline logo in headline).
- Hotel reservations in the Schengen member states (Must be mentioned hotel address and all guests names in English or German only) and provide confirmation letter from each hotel in Schengen Area.
- Invitation letter from inviting company in Austria (period of stay, purpose of visit etc).
- Copy of the Family Card, Marriage Certificate and Birth Certificate (when traveling with family).
Documents provided by the inviting company:
- if the applicant has not enough own financial means: Electronic guarantee letter, which can be applied for at the local police station in Austria, where the company is located/registered. For more information please visit
https://www.bmi.gv.at/202/Fremdenpolizei_und_Grenzkontrolle/Einreise/start.aspx#eve (https://www.bmi.gv.at/202/Fremdenpolizei_und_Grenzkontrolle/Einreise/start.aspx#eve)
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant or submission of falsified or forged documents result in the refusal of the visa application.

## For Malta
Documents provided by the applicant:
- Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photographs, (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted, person must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid 3 months longer than the expiry date of the requested visa).
- Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa (if available)
- Original Letter from employer and/or sponsor stating designation, date of employment, salary, period of stay, purpose of visit, stating full responsibility for employee’s expenses during trip and his/her return stamped and signed by the General Manager (name should also be printed), and copy of SIUP or TDP (self-employed applicants should present trade/business license, tax documents etc). Copy of Doctor Certification (Surat Ijin Praktek or Ikatan Dokter Indonesia card) in case medical doctor. And Original Pay Slip for the last 3 months
- Original Personal bank account statements must cover the last 3 months and need to be under the applicant’s name. Families travelling together also need to submit bank statements for the last 3 months of every travelling family member who has a bank account, except minors (under 18 years old). In addition, if applicant’s expenses are covered by a family member or company, the respective sponsor’s last 3 months bank statements, financial sponsorship letter and photocopy of sponsor’s ID Card must be submitted with each application. Original Bank account statement must be legalized by Bank.
- Travel insurance (minimum coverage 30,000) policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company and should cover covid. (Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies.
- Flight reservation (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only) (Flight reservation must be issued by Airline with airline logo in headline).
- Hotel reservations in the Schengen member states (Must be mentioned hotel address and all guests names in English or German only) and provide confirmation letter from each hotel in Schengen Area.
- Invitation letter from inviting company in Malta (period of stay, purpose of visit etc).
- Copy of the Family Card, Marriage Certificate and Birth Certificate (when traveling with family).
Documents provided by the inviting company:
If the applicant has not enough own financial means:
- A signed invitation letter from the company in Malta explaining the purpose and duration of the visit and including, if applicable, financial responsibility for the stay in Malta.
- Proof of sufficient financial means.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant or submission of falsified or forged documents result in the refusal of the visa application.

## For Slovenia
Documents provided by the applicant:
- Visa application form, completed and signed, with 2 attached recent photographs, (photo must be: 35 mm wide, 45 mm high, photo must be well contrasted, person must look straight into the camera, head must be straight, not tilted, mouth closed, head must take up 2/3 of the photo, but must not exceed 36 mm in height, eyes must be in the upper middle of the photo, eye color must be clearly recognizable, distance between the two pupils must be between 8 and 10 mm, white background please).
- Current, valid passport (the passport must be valid 3 months longer than the expiry date of the requested visa).
- Photocopy of the first and end page of the passport and Schengen Visa (if available)
- Original Letter from employer and/or sponsor stating designation, date of employment, salary, period of stay, purpose of visit, stating full responsibility for employee’s expenses during trip and his/her return stamped and signed by the General Manager (name should also be printed), and copy of SIUP or TDP (self-employed applicants should present trade/business license, tax documents etc). Copy of Doctor Certification (Surat Ijin Praktek or Ikatan Dokter Indonesia card) in case medical doctor. And Original Pay Slip for the last 3 months
- Original Personal bank account statements must cover the last 3 months and need to be under the applicant’s name. Families travelling together also need to submit bank statements for the last 3 months of every travelling family member who has a bank account, except minors (under 18 years old). In addition, if applicant’s expenses are covered by a family member or company, the respective sponsor’s last 3 months bank statements, financial sponsorship letter and photocopy of sponsor’s ID Card must be submitted with each application. Original Bank account statement must be legalized by Bank.
- Travel insurance (minimum coverage 30,000) policy must be issued by an accredited insurance company and should cover covid. (Please click here to see enclosed list of accredited insurance companies.
- Flight reservation (ticket has to be presented to the Embassy upon request only) (Flight reservation must be issued by Airline with airline logo in headline).
- Hotel reservations in the Schengen member states (Must be mentioned hotel address and all guests names in English or German only) and provide confirmation letter from each hotel in Schengen Area.
- Invitation letter from inviting company in Slovenia (period of stay, purpose of visit etc).
- Copy of the Family Card, Marriage Certificate and Birth Certificate (when traveling with family).
Documents provided by the inviting company:
- if the applicant has not enough own financial means:
- The letter of guarantee may be issued only by a legal entity based in the Republic of Slovenia. The letter of guarantee must clearly state the signatory’s full name.
- The letter of guarantee should be original.
- The legal entity is required to enclose documents with the letter of guarantee substantiating statements written in the letter of guarantee:
- proof of preliminary business cooperation: invoices, customs declarations etc.;
- valid contracts in accordance with which legal business is done.

The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents. The application form can only be processed after complete submission of all documents requested by the Embassy. The non-refundable visa fee has to be paid upon filing the application.
Important: Wrong statements by the applicant or submission of falsified or forged documents result in the refusal of the visa application.
Note : all documents must be printed in A4 format.
